step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture diagram features a user portal hosted on S3 and served via CloudFront. Authentication is handled by Cognito integrating with Sanofi SSO. Dynamic tracking and logging are routed from S3 to API Gateway and DynamoDB. Safe environments are provisioned via Service Catalog, which deploys RStudio (running on EC2) and SageMaker. NLP workflows run on Lambda, invoking Translate and Comprehend Medical. A bottom S3 bucket acts as the secure Data Lake.

graph.name: Sanofi Darwin Real World Evidence Platform
graph.notes: A secure, segregated data science portal on AWS allowing 30-40 analysts to safely provision analytical environments and perform NLP text translation and clinical entity recognition.

nodes.name: Data Scientists
nodes.notes: Sanofi data analysts accessing the RWE platform.

nodes.name: CloudFront CDN
nodes.notes: Secures traffic and caches the static portal website.

nodes.name: Cognito
nodes.notes: Manages user identity federation with corporate SSO.

nodes.name: DynamoDB
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores portal metadata and audit trails of user actions.

nodes.name: Service Catalog
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Deploys standardized, pre-configured resources via CloudFormation.

nodes.name: RStudio
nodes.notes: RStudio workspace provisioned on EC2 with encrypted storage.

nodes.name: SageMaker Notebooks
nodes.notes: Secured SageMaker notebooks for ML workloads.

nodes.name: Lambda
nodes.notes: Orchestrates text translation and analysis requests.

nodes.name: Translate
nodes.notes: Translates raw data files into target languages.

nodes.name: Comprehend Medical
nodes.notes: Identifies medical entities and clinical information.

nodes.name: Data Lake
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Highly sensitive raw data and analytical outputs.

edges.notes: Logs API actions and audit details.
